input.background: The Youth Risk Behavior Surveillance System monitors six categories of health behaviors in U.S. youth and adolescents. Many types of behaviors that contribute to adverse health consequences are monitored.
input.question: As examples, the proportion of high school students who had ridden with a driver who had been drinking alcohol during the preceding 30-day period was $30.2 \%$, the proportion who had smoked cigarettes was $21.9 \%$, and the proportion who were overweight was $13.5 \%$. These values are statistical estimates of population parameters.

Proportion of students who rode with a d.id: Proportion of students who rode with a driver who had been drinking
Proportion of students who rode with a d.description: The percentage of high school students who rode with a driver who had been drinking alcohol during the preceding 30-day period.

Proportion of students who smoked cigare.id: Proportion of students who smoked cigarettes
Proportion of students who smoked cigare.description: The percentage of high school students who smoked cigarettes during the preceding 30-day period.

Proportion of students who were overweig.id: Proportion of students who were overweight
Proportion of students who were overweig.description: The percentage of high school students who were considered overweight.
